decisions.rationale: „Eigentor zugeben“ liest sich stärker als Fairness/Ehrlichkeit als als Teamgeist. Ein klar kooperatives Team-Item behebt die Mehrdeutigkeit ohne Strukturänderung.
"Fork" is a tactic, not a special move. Replacing it with a castling variant keeps the group clearly chess-specific and accessible.
The herding-dog group is weakened by generic type terms. Replacing the non-breed labels with specific breed names makes the set cleaner.
"Compass" creates an unnecessary cue overlap with map-reading. A more distinct hiking item keeps the categories separate.
"Relay" is too broad for a sprint group. A specific sprint relay event makes the category fairer.
The overlap is systemic across multiple groups defined by use/occasion rather than distinct shoe types. A fair fix would require reworking several categories, not a minimal patch.
"Fruit Trees" substantially overlaps with "Deciduous Trees," and the tropical/fruit split is also blurry. Fixing this cleanly would require a broader category redesign.
The category boundaries are broadly fuzzy across formal, sun, warm, and sport headwear. A reliable fix needs more than one-item swaps and would effectively restructure the puzzle.

The Chinese/Japanese culture groups are weakened by items with broad East Asian associations. Replacing the least specific entries with clearer national cues improves fairness while preserving structure.
"Castle" and "Forest" are too generic for specifically Arthurian places. Replacing them with named Arthurian locations makes the set materially fairer.
The steam-power and transport groups overlap at the category level, not just on one item. A fair repair would require rethinking at least one full group.
"Focaccia" is plausibly both a flatbread and a leavened bread. Replacing it with a more clearly flat-only bread resolves the ambiguity minimally.
"Orca" is a whale, creating an avoidable species/subgroup overlap within the pod category. A sibling marine mammal keeps the group cleaner.
"Bowstring Arch Bridge" overlaps too closely with tied-arch terminology for an easy puzzle. Replacing it with a more distinct arch subtype improves separation.
The entire behavioural-economics group substantially overlaps with cognitive biases. A clean fix would require replacing or redefining the whole category.
